data.title: China Commerce Minister 'Strongly' Slams US Tariffs in Letter to US Counterpart
data.description:
Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao "strongly" criticized US tariffs in his congratulatory letter to newly confirmed US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, the ministry said Wednesday.

Wang congratulated his US counterpart, but then proceeded to say the US' action to unilaterally impose tariffs "has undermined the normal economic and trade cooperation between China and the US," according to the ministry.

Wang said Beijing will work with Washington "to strengthen dialogue, manage differences, promote cooperation, and create a fair and predictable policy environment" to help create a "fair and predictable policy environment."

US President Donald Trump imposed a 10% tariff on Chinese imports at the beginning of February as Trump said Beijing needed to stop the flow of the opioid to the US.

China and the US have cooperated in the control of fentanyl and "achieved remarkable results," the ministry said.

data.title: Market Chatter: Ex-ICBC Banker Gets Death Sentence in China's Anti-Graft Crackdown
data.description:
China sentenced former Industrial and Commercial Bank of China banker Zhang Hongli to death with a two-year reprieve for bribery, Xinhua News Agency reported Wednesday.

The Hangzhou Intermediate People's Court of China's Zhejiang province found that Zhang used his position to provide loan financing and other job arrangements totaling more than 177 million yuan.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Lemon Tree Hotels Terminates Agreement for Red Fox Hotel in Uttarakhand, India
data.description:
Lemon Tree Hotels and its wholly-owned unit Carnation Hotels have mutually terminated their agreements with Hotel Mahanand for the Red Fox by Lemon Tree Hotels at Neelkanth in Uttarakhand, India, the company said in a Wednesday filing with the Indian exchanges.

The termination covers the hotel operating and license agreements signed in April 2018 and an addendum from February 2022.

Lemon Tree said the termination is not expected to have a significant impact on its net worth.

data.title: India's Waaree Energies gains on solar module order
data.description:
** Shares of Waaree Energies rise 3.4% to 2,295 rupees

** Co did not specify the financial terms of the deal

** Stock up 9% so far this week, eyes best week since Dec. 9

data.title: News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Thursday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
HSBC Plans New Share Buyback, Cost Cuts

The London-based bank's chief executive is concentrating on streamlining the lender's businesses and reducing costs.

NAB's Earnings Hit by Higher Business-Loan Impairments

Chief Executive Andrew Irvine said the economic outlook is improving, "but cost of living and interest rate challenges persisted."

A Lingering Question at Coinbase: Who Really Owns the Customers' Crypto?

Coinbase's accounting change significantly affects its numbers and highlights an old worry.

Activist Launches Proxy Fight at National Health Investors

Land & Buildings argues the REIT is too closely tied to a large tenant and could get better lease terms.

Ackman Boosts Bid for Howard Hughes, Vowing to Create Modern Version of Berkshire Hathaway

The revised proposal would increase Pershing Square's stake to 48%.

Fidelity Investments Reports Record Annual Profit, Revenue

The stock market's rally drew more money into the firm's funds and spurred trading activity by its customers.

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Read about insuring European banks, Bank Indonesia, ASR Nederland and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

Shift4 Strikes $1.5 Billion Deal for Tax-Free Shopping Business

The expected $7.50-per-share offer for Global Blue Group represents a 15% premium to where the tax-free shopping services provider's shares closed last week.

Argentine President's Promotion of Crypto Rocks His Presidency

An ally of Trump and darling of the global right, Javier Milei tries to deflect critics after a cryptocurrency he endorsed collapsed, wiping out $4 billion in value.

Neuberger Berman Raises $1.6 Billion for Asset-Based Credit Bets

The asset manager has already invested about 45% of its latest specialty-finance fund, which took almost two years to raise.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Thursday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Arctic blast sends natural gas to highest since 2022, and oil ticks up on supply worries

Natural-gas futures surged Wednesday, finishing at the highest in over two years, as an Arctic blast across much of the U.S. boosted demand and also appeared to weigh on production.

Activist Elliott to Seek Phillips 66 Board Seats

With a more-than-$2.5 billion stake in Phillips 66, Elliott has pushed for a streamlining of the oil refiner and seeks more representation on the company's board.

Occidental Stock Drops After Mixed Results. What's Dragging on Buffett's Favorite.

OXY stock has lost 19% over the past year. Weak crude prices are to blame.

Diamondback Energy to Acquire Double Eagle

The oil and natural gas company reached an agreement with Double Eagle in exchange for 6.9 million shares of its common stock and $3 billion in cash.

Galp Misses Expectations; Launches Buyback

The oil-and-gas producer launched a $262 million share buyback and said its results reflected a strong performance despite a volatile economic environment.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Nutrien, Orsted, Woodside Energy, Diamondback Energy and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

New Sanctions for Putin's 'Shadow Fleet' Are on the Horizon

The EU is ready to roll out new sanctions on vessels transporting illicit oil, but the Trump administration has yet to say if it will participate.

Clean-Energy Program Fueled by Biden Takes Deep Staff Cuts Under Trump

The number of employees in the Energy Department's loan office has been sliced by roughly 25%.

Eneos Plans to Spin off Metals Subsidiary

The energy company plans to spin off its metals subsidiary and raise as much as $3 billion through a listing that would be one of Japan's biggest such offerings in recent years.

Trump Anxiety Could Boost Sales at These Renewables Companies

The rush to purchase renewable energy equipment before clean energy laws get changed could juice the results of companies that make that equipment.

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 28 ringgit to 4,644 ringgit a ton. "Palm Oil Falls, Tracking Soybean Oil Weakness — Market Talk," at 0238 GMT, incorrectly said it fell 63 ringgit to 4,567 ringgit a ton.

0244 GMT - Iron ore prices edge higher in early Asian trading amid improving investor sentiment. China's property sector has shown further signs of recovery, with the decline in new-home prices easing for a fifth month in January. New-home prices in 70 cities fell 0.07% from December, according to NBS data. "The figures offer a glimmer of hope for the property sector, which is the biggest consumer of steel and iron ore," ANZ Research says in a note. That comes after the CEO of BHP, the world's largest listed miner, said there are tentative signs of a recovery in China's property sector. The most-traded iron ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 0.1% higher at 819.00 yuan a ton. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0238 GMT - Palm oil prices fall in Asian trading, mirroring weakness in soybean oil overnight on the Chicago Board of Trade. However, palm oil prices are expected to stay firm in the near term, supported by limited supply, Abdul Hameed, director of sales at Pakistan-based Manzoor Trading says. He notes that rising demand ahead of festivals, including Ramadan, especially in the Central Asia region, and shrinking global inventories, may support prices. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 28 ringgit to 4,644 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com) Corrections & Amplifications

This market talk item was corrected at 0249 GMT to reflect the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 28 ringgit to 4,644 ringgit a ton. The original version incorrectly said it fell 63 ringgit to 4,567 ringgit a ton

0233 GMT - Copper is little changed in early Asian trading. Markets seem to be adopting a wait-and-see approach following a flurry of volatility stemming from Trump's tariff threats, ANZ Research analysts say in commentary. The specter of higher levies on copper imports into the U.S. will likely drive some upward pressure on prices, the analysts say. However, ANZ says that impact could be offset by potential softening in global economic growth should a global trade war emerge. The three-month LME copper contract is flat at $9,463.50 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0009 GMT - Gold edges higher in early Asian trade, as investors digest the Fed's latest policy meeting minutes. The precious metal appears to be supported by the central bank's minutes, with the Fed uncertain what the economic environment will look like in terms of inflation. Escalating trade tensions have also increased demand for safe-haven assets like gold, says Harbourfront Wealth - Sonora Wealth Group in a note. Trump's recent proposal to impose an additional 25% on auto imports has raised market volatility, with major economies including China expected to impose retaliatory measures, investment advisor Miro Svoboda says. Spot gold is up 0.1% at $2,937.03/oz.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

2306 GMT - The market will appreciate some moderation in Fortescue's spending on energy projects, Citi analyst Paul McTaggart says in a note. The iron-ore miner earlier pared FY25 energy capex guidance to US$400 million from US$500 million. "That said, market will want to see energy capex and opex cut further," McTaggart says. Citi has a neutral rating and A$21.00 target on Fortescue. McTaggart reckons the miner's 1H fiscal result will be neutral to mildly negative for its shares. The stock last traded at A$19.45/share. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2303 GMT - Rio Tinto's cost guidance disappoints RBC Capital Markets analyst Kaan Peker, who was expecting a bigger improvement in costs given the ramp up in volumes at the Oyu Tolgoi and Kennecott mines. In a note, Peker also raises concerns about first ore from Simandou, expected later this year, and the possible impact that will have on the iron-ore market. Otherwise, Rio Tinto is delivering on its strategy and has one of the better growth rates among major miners, with a focus on copper, iron ore and lithium, Peker says. RBC has a sector perform rating and A$120 target on Rio Tinto, which last traded at A$121.95/share. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2146 GMT - Rio Tinto's dividend beat is a small positive in an overall in-line set of annual fiscal results, Citi analyst Ephrem Ravi says in a note. The miner's total dividend of $4.02/share is a 4% above consensus expectations as compiled by Visible Alpha. Otherwise, Ebitda is as expected, while 2025 production and capital expenditure guidance is reiterated, Ravi says. To be sure, net debt is higher than expected, he says. Citi has a neutral rating and GBP56.00 target on the stock, which closed in London at GBP50.36. In Sydney, Rio Tinto ended Wednesday at A$121.95/share. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1931 GMT - Oil futures post moderate gains on supply concerns including this week's drone strike on a pumping station for a pipeline carrying crude from Kazakhstan to the Black Sea, and some North Dakota production being shut in by the freezing weather hitting much of the U.S. The market will be watching for a fourth consecutively weekly build in U.S. crude stockpiles in the EIA's report Thursday. Gasoline stocks are seen unchanged, and distillate stocks down, according to a Wall Street Journal survey of analysts. WTI settles up 0.6% at $72.25 a barrel, and Brent rises 0.3% to $75.67 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1930 GMT - Front-month gold futures settle down 0.4% to $2,919.40 an ounce. It makes it four sessions out of the past six that front-month gold finishes lower. But gold did start the day on the rise, fueled by the continued demand for gold as a safe haven asset to hedge against volatile markets, says Robert Yawger of Mizuho Securities USA in a note. Today's Fed minutes also appear to give gold support, with the Fed uncertain as to what the economic environment will look like in terms of inflation going forward. SPDR Gold erase earlier losses to trade flat. (kirk. maltais@wsj.com)

1918 GMT - U.S. natural gas futures rise for a seventh consecutive session as Arctic weather across much of the U.S. lifts demand and freezes in some production, while forecasts show winter stretching into March with large storage withdrawals widening inventory deficits. EIA data due tomorrow are expected to show a 187 Bcf draw from underground storage, leaving inventories at 2,110 Bcf, or 109 Bcf below the five-year average and 377 Bcf below the year-earlier level, according to a Wall Street Journal survey of analysts. The Nymex front month settles up 6.8% at $4.280/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1859 GMT - Lean hog futures on the CME close down 3.8% to 90.425 cents a pound. The drop came as pork cutout prices reported by the USDA fell through midday, as well as through yesterday. Pork carcass cutouts fell $3.10 per hundredweight through midday, down to $96.37 per cwt. Livestock traders are now asking questions about if pork demand is still strong, or if cutouts have more room to move lower, says independent livestock trader Dan Norcini. "Pork remains cheap compared to beef," says Norcini. Live cattle futures finish up 0.4% to $1.9515 a pound. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Eniro Group Explores Spinoff of Dynava Business
data.description:
Eniro Group said Wednesday it launched a review of the potential separate listing of its Dynava business.

Dynava provides contact center solutions in Sweden, Finland and Norway, catering to public and private clients. It accounts for more than a third of the Swedish software company's sales.

The group tapped mergers and acquisitions and capital markets advisor Carlsquare to oversee the review. A proposal will be presented to Eniro shareholders if the group decides to push through with the separate listing.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Trump Tariffs, Geopolitics Weigh
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

Eurozone flash consumer confidence; trading updates for Zurich Insurance, Mercedes-Benz, Swedbank, Leonardo, Centrica, Repsol, Lloyds Banking Group, Airbus, Schneider Electric, Renault, Anglo American, Nebius, NN Group

Opening Call:

European stock futures edged higher, after Asia stocks traded mixed. U.S. Treasury yields were flat while the dollar slightly weakened. Gold rose while oil futures were mixed.

Equities:

Equity futures in Europe pointed to a higher open as investors further digested fresh tariff threats from President Trump and his latest comments calling Ukranian President Volodymyr Zelensky a "dictator."

"Even as tariff policy shifts, there are certain industries that are clearly within its crosshairs," said Kara Murphy, chief investment officer at Kestra Investment Management.

In addition, minutes from the Federal Reserve's January meeting revealed little appetite for near-term interest rate cuts, while pointing to strong consumer demand and changes to trade and immigration policy as potential sources that could hinder recent progress on inflation.

Forex:

The euro-yen pair is forming a major bearish reversal pattern, based on the daily chart, according to Oanda's Kelvin Wong.

Its swing highs seen in November 2023, July and October 2024 have formed an impending major bearish reversal "head and shoulders" pattern, said the senior market analyst.

This comes after the pair's break below the support on a former long-term ascending channel in August, he said.

A daily close below the 155.45 level could open the scope to trigger a major multimonth downtrend that may expose the next medium-term support levels of 151.00 and 145.60, the analyst added.

Bonds:

The yield on the U.S. 10-Year Note was steady at lower levels, which typically decreases the appeal of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback as traders likely digested various risks in the market.

While Trump's tariff threats still loom, it remains unclear whether the tariffs will actually be enacted or be used as negotiating tools, said Maybank analysts.

In addition, a peace deal for Ukraine remains uncertain, particularly with Trump calling Ukrainian President Zelensky a "dictator without elections," the analysts note.

Energy:

Oil futures were mixed as traders assessed the prospect of global tariffs.

"Tariffs and counter-tariffs have the potential to weigh on the oil-intensive part of the global economy in particular, creating uncertainty over demand," Morgan Stanley Research's Serena Tang said.

However, this uncertainty would likely also prompt OPEC+ to extend current production cuts once again, which would create a balanced market in 2H, said the chief global cross-asset strategist.

Metals:

Gold edged higher in early Asian trade, as investors digested the Fed's latest policy meeting minutes.

Escalating trade tensions have also increased demand for safe-haven assets like gold, said Harbourfront Wealth - Sonora Wealth Group.

Trump's recent proposal to impose an additional 25% on auto imports has raised market volatility, with major economies including China expected to impose retaliatory measures, investment advisor Miro Svoboda added.

-

Copper was little changed as markets seemed to be adopting a wait-and-see approach following a flurry of volatility stemming from Trump's tariff threats, ANZ Research analysts said.

The specter of higher levies on copper imports into the U.S. will likely drive some upward pressure on prices, the analysts added.

ANZ said that the impact could be offset by potential softening in global economic growth should a global trade war emerge.

-

Iron ore prices edged higher amid improving investor sentiment.

China's property sector has shown further signs of recovery, with the decline in new-home prices easing for a fifth month in January.

New-home prices data offers a glimmer of hope for the property sector, which is the biggest consumer of steel and iron ore," ANZ Research said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Fed Minutes Reveal Little Appetite for Near-Term Rate Cuts

Fed officials were broadly comfortable with their decision to hold interest rates steady at their meeting last month and offered nothing to suggest any immediate change to their wait-and-see stance on interest-rate cuts.

"A majority of participants observed that the current high degree of uncertainty made it appropriate for the committee to take a careful approach in considering additional adjustments to the stance of monetary policy," said the minutes of the Jan. 28-29 policy meeting, which were published Wednesday with a customary three-week lag.

Trump's Attack on Zelensky Signals New World Order Taking Shape

President Trump has dramatically shifted the direction of U.S. foreign policy in four short weeks, making the U.S. a less reliable ally and retreating from global commitments in ways that stand to fundamentally reshape America's relationship with the world.

His top envoys have floated concessions to Russia in peace talks that stunned European allies, followed by Trump calling Ukraine's leader a dictator, and he kept Europeans at arms length as the negotiations began. He has dismantled the leading U.S. aid agency providing assistance to the developing world where China aims to establish a foothold. Trump's plan to own Gaza and remove Palestinians from the enclave erased decades of Washington's efforts to broker a two-state solution. And his plans to increase tariffs heralded an end to American-fueled globalization.

Rio Tinto 2024 Profit Rises, but Weaker China Iron-Ore Demand Hurts Underlying Earnings

Rio Tinto reported a 15% rise in annual net profit, but pared its dividend as weaker iron-ore prices weighed on underlying earnings.

The world's second-biggest miner by market value recorded a net profit of $11.55 billion in 2024, up from $10.06 billion in 2023. Its bottom line benefited from some asset sales and lower impairment charges against its Australian alumina refineries.

Lenovo Doubles Profit on Strong Sales

Lenovo Group reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings, with device sales strong, boosted by its bet on the fast-growing use of artificial intelligence.

Net profit for the three months ended December more than doubled to $693 million, the world's largest PC maker said Thursday, beating the $381.7 million estimate in a FactSet poll of analysts. The result was partly boosted by a $282 million nonrecurring income tax credit.

X Hinted at Possible Deal Trouble in Talks With Ad Giant to Increase Spending

A lawyer at advertising conglomerate Interpublic Group fielded a phone call in December from a lawyer at X.The message was clear, according to multiple people with knowledge of the conversation: Get your clients to spend more on Elon Musk's social-media platform, or else.

X CEO Linda Yaccarino has made comments that seemed like similar warnings in conversations with Interpublic executives, according to people with knowledge of those talks.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Thursday

00:01/UK: BRC Consumer Sentiment Monitor

05:30/NED: Jan Unemployment

05:30/NED: Feb Consumer confidence survey

07:00/SWI: Jan Trade Balance

07:00/SWE: 4Q Industrial capacity utilization

07:00/DEN: 4Q Preliminary GDP

07:00/DEN: Feb Consumer expectations

07:00/NOR: 4Q Labour Cost Index - preliminary figures

07:00/DEN: 4Q Labour force survey

07:00/GER: Jan PPI

09:00/POL: Jan PPI

09:00/POL: Jan Industrial Production Index

11:00/FRA: 4Q OECD Quarterly National Accounts: GDP growth

11:00/IRL: 4Q Labour Force Survey

15:00/EU: Feb FCCI Flash Consumer Confidence Indicator

15:59/GRE: Dec Balance of Payments

17:59/POR: Dec ICSG Copper Report

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: Palm Oil Trades Slightly Lower
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures fell modestly, hovering below MYR 4,680 per tonne, after a near 4% surge in the prior session, as some traders locked in profits ahead of a major industry conference.

Caution also grew ahead of export estimates for the first 20 days of February from cargo surveyors.

Meantime, US President Trump said he would launch more tariffs “over the next month or sooner,” fueling concerns across various markets, including palm oil.

Simultaneously, Kuala Lumpur kept its March export tax for crude palm oil at 10% but cut the reference price to MYR 4,390.37.

Still, concerns over supply capped further losses.

This followed Malaysia’s meteorological agency forecast of heavy rain in some palm-producing states until February 25.

Further, worker productivity may decline during Ramadan and the Eid-ul-Fitr celebrations.

In top producer Indonesia, the government is exploring a 50% palm oil biodiesel blend for 2026 and a 3% jet fuel blend, potentially influencing future demand.

data.title: China Launches Action Plan to Bolster Foreign Investment
data.description:
China introduced an action plan to boost the country's foreign investment for this year, Xinhua reported Wednesday, citing an issue from the General Office of the State Council.

Under the plan, new opening-up policies will be implemented in industries such as telecommunication, biotechnology and medical services, the report said.

Restrictions on domestic loans for foreign-invested firms will also be lifted to attract more international investments, the report said.

The Ministry of Commerce and the National Development and Reform Commission composed the plan, which was approved at a State Council executive meeting, the report said.

data.title: Baidu Likely to Benefit From Strong AI Business — Market Talk
data.description: Baidu is likely to benefit from strong AI business, say HSBC analysts in a commentary. The tech company's 4Q cloud revenue came in "meaningfully ahead of consensus" on stronger application programming interface demand, the analysts say. HSBC expects this growth momentum to sustain in 2025, with rising AI adoption from customers and cross-selling opportunities for AI infrastructure in the coming months. HSBC maintains a hold rating and raises target price of its ADRs slightly to US$94.00 from US$93.00. The ADRs last traded at US$88.03, while the Hong Kong-listed shares are down 2.7% at HK$85.95. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Whitecap Resources Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Whitecap Resources Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 40 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 49 cents. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 21 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 14 cents to 28 cents per share.

  • Revenue was C$852.40 million​; analysts expected C$795.00 million.

  • Whitecap Resources Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 40 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of C$233.8 million.

  • Whitecap Resources Inc shares had fallen by 3.2% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 11 "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the oil & gas exploration and production peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Whitecap Resources Inc is C$14.00

data.title: BNB Price Approaches a Key Level—Can It Clear the Hurdle?
data.description:
BNB price is recovering from the $630 support zone. The price is rising and might aim for a fresh move toward the $680 resistance.

  • BNB price is moving higher above the $650 resistance zone.
  • The price is now trading below $660 and the 100-hourly simple moving average.
  • There is a connecting bearish trend line forming with resistance at $655 on the hourly chart of the BNB/USD pair (data source from Binance).
  • The pair must stay above the $650 level to start another increase in the near term.

BNB Price Eyes Fresh Upside

After forming a base above the $630 level, BNB price started a fresh increase. There was a move above the $635 and $640 resistance levels. It even recovered above the $650 level, beating Ethereum and Bitcoin.

There was a decent move above the 50% Fib retracement level of the downward move from the $682 swing high to the $630 low. However, the bears seem to be active near the $655 and $660 levels. There is also a connecting bearish trend line forming with resistance at $655 on the hourly chart of the BNB/USD pair.

The price is now trading below $660 and the 100-hourly simple moving average. If there is a fresh increase, the price could face resistance near the $655 level. The next resistance sits near the $660 level or the 61.8% Fib retracement level of the downward move from the $682 swing high to the $630 low at $662.

A clear move above the $662 zone could send the price higher. In the stated case, BNB price could test $680. A close above the $680 resistance might set the pace for a larger move toward the $700 resistance. Any more gains might call for a test of the $720 level in the near term.

Another Decline?

If BNB fails to clear the $662 resistance, it could start another decline. Initial support on the downside is near the $650 level. The next major support is near the $642 level.

The main support sits at $630. If there is a downside break below the $630 support, the price could drop toward the $612 support. Any more losses could initiate a larger decline toward the $600 level.

Technical Indicators

Hourly MACD – The MACD for BNB/USD is gaining pace in the bullish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for BNB/USD is currently above the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $650 and $642.

Major Resistance Levels – $655 and $662.

data.title: Elon Musk is not India’s ideal foreign investor
data.description:
The country's high import levies have failed to get the Tesla boss to make his vehicles locally. New Delhi may be considering concessions and exemptions to entice him instead. But Donald Trump's trade wars leave India with less leverage to wrangle coveted factory jobs from Musk.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @ShritamaBose on X

CONTEXT NEWS

U.S. President Donald Trump told Fox News in an interview aired on February 19 that if Tesla were to build a factory in India to circumvent that country's tariffs, it would be "unfair" to the United States.

Tesla has selected locations for two showrooms in New Delhi and Mumbai, moving closer to its long-delayed plans to sell its electric cars in India, Reuters reported on February 18, citing unnamed sources familiar with the matter.

The electric vehicle maker has posted job ads on professional networking platform LinkedIn for 15 positions across three locations in India since February 18.

data.title: Shin Shin Names Chairman
data.description:
Shin Shin appointed Lee Wen Ying as the company's chairman following a board resolution on Wednesday, according to a same-day Taiwan Exchange filing.

Lee succeeds You Zhi Huang in the role, the construction machinery company said.

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on February 20
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Thursday.

The lira was quoted at 36.3200-36.3245 against the U.S. dollar at 0450 GMT, after closing at 36.2975 on Wednesday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 1.55% lower at 9,763 points on Wednesday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian shares fell sharply on Thursday, tracking choppy trading on Wall Street overnight and a dip in European stocks as U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff plans and a cautious stance from Federal Reserve policymakers hurt risk sentiment.

FOREIGN MINISTER

Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan will attend a G20 foreign ministers meeting in Johannesburg.

TRANSPORT MINISTER

Transport Minister Abdulkadir Uraloglu will attend an event of a university in Ankara (0700 GMT).

OPPOSITION

Main opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel will visit jailed mayors, a journalist and the leader of another small opposition party who were detained in the latest investigations against opposition figures in Istanbul (1200 GMT).

CONSUMER CONFIDENCE (TRCONC=ECI)

The Turkish Statistics Institute will announce February consumer confidence index data (0700 GMT).

DEBT STOCK

Turkish treasury will announce debt stock for January (1430 GMT).

CENTRAL BANK DATA

The Central Bank will release its weekly data, including on forex reserves (1130 GMT).

BUSINESS GROUP PROBE

An Istanbul court banned on Thursday international travel by two key executives of Turkey's top business association in an investigation of their remarks on democracy that President Tayyip Erdogan characterised as undermining the government.

EGG EXPORT

Turkey has begun exporting around 15,000 tonnes of eggs to the United States as a devastating outbreak of bird flu is slashing U.S. production and sending prices soaring, a leading sector official said on Wednesday.

IRAQ PIPELINE

Turkey has yet to receive confirmation from Iraq on the resumption of oil flows through the Iraq-Turkey pipeline, Turkish Energy Minister Alparslan Bayraktar told Reuters on Wednesday.

data.title: Wesfarmers Will Continue to Evaluate Business Case for Lithium Expansion, CEO Says
Wesfarmers Has Capacity to Expand Lithium Project, CEO Says
Wesfarmers Focused on Ramping Up Lithium Refinery, CEO Says — Interview
data.description:
(05:10 GMT) *Wesfarmers Has Capacity to Expand Lithium Project, CEO Says
(05:10 GMT) *Wesfarmers Will Continue to Evaluate Business Case for Lithium Expansion, CEO Says
(05:10 GMT) *Target Customers for Lithium Project Are Non-Chinese Battery, Car Manufacturers, CEO Says
(05:10 GMT) Wesfarmers Focused on Ramping Up Lithium Refinery, CEO Says — Interview

By Mike Cherney

Australian retail-industrial conglomerate Wesfarmers is focused on ramping up a refinery at its lithium joint venture in Western Australia, and a possible expansion of the project is still on the table despite a drop in lithium prices, Chief Executive Rob Scott said in an interview.

"Over the last couple of years, we have acknowledged that we have the capacity to expand the project, given that we have a very high-quality and long-life mine," Scott told The Wall Street Journal. "But we've been very consistent in saying that we'll continue to evaluate the business case."

"There's certainly no change to how we would assess that expansion," he added, noting that getting the refinery commissioned and ramping up production is the company's first-order priority.

Prices for lithium, needed for electric-vehicle batteries, have experienced a sharp fall from record highs over the past two years. But producers continue to plan new projects, betting the metal will be in demand for EVs and energy storage for decades to come.

Wesfarmers's lithium project, a joint venture with Chilean mining company SQM, includes a mine and concentrator that produce a raw form called spodumene, plus a refinery that will make battery-grade lithium hydroxide.

Wesfarmers, which reported half-year earnings Thursday, said earlier in the day that refinery construction was 95% complete, commissioning was about 50% complete and that it expects its first product from the refinery in mid-calendar year 2025.

Like many critical minerals, China plays an outsize role in processing lithium. But extra tariffs on Chinese imports from President Trump could make lithium refined in other countries more attractive to customers seeking to limit exposure to China in their supply chains.

"Our target customer base are essentially non-Chinese battery manufacturers and car manufacturers," Scott said. "Whatever happens with the tariffs, we would expect our lithium would be sought after by a lot of non-Chinese producers."

Although there is uncertainty about how tariffs would impact the global economy, Scott told reporters earlier in the day that he didn't think tariffs would directly affect Wesfarmers's lithium business. When asked if the lithium business would make an adequate return if fully operational, he said it would.

"If we just look at our concentrator, would we be profitable selling spodumene at the market price when our concentrator is fully ramped? The answer to that would be yes," he told reporters. "Would we expect to be profitable through our lithium hydroxide refinery while it is ramped up based on the contracts we have and where the market's at? Yes we would."

"Clearly, if there were improvements in the future in price, then that would be beneficial," he added.

In 2023, Wesfarmers said its share of capital expenditure for the project would be 1.2 billion Australian dollars to A$1.3 billion, equivalent to US$761.4 million to US$824.8 million, excluding capitalized interest.

Scott was speaking after Wesfarmers reported a 2.9% rise in its half-year profit. The result was driven by a strong performance in its Bunnings and Kmart retail chains, which make up the bulk of Wesfarmers's earnings, though the company often invests in other businesses that it believes have strong growth prospects.

Write to Mike Cherney at mike.cherney@wsj.com

Shareholders that lost money on Construction Partners, Inc.(ROAD) should contact Levi & Korsinsky about Securities Fraud Investigation - ROAD
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 19, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Construction Partners, Inc. ("Construction Partners, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On January 23, 2025, Spruce Point Capital Management published a report highlighting the increasing pressures faced by Construction Partners suggesting that the share price could have a 35-50% potential long-term downside risk. The Spruce Point report focused on Construction Partners' major decline in contracts from its largest customer, Florida Department of Transportation, and a weak expansion into Texas, which was portrayed as a strong acquisition for the company. The report concluded that the market had miscalculated Construction Partners' enterprise value, which in turn, reflects a misunderstanding of the company's business and financial prospects, thus, predicting the 35-50% downside potential.

Following this news, Construction Partners' stock price plummeted over 8% to open at $85.17 per share on January 23, 2025 To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/construction-partners-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=130792&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. data.title: S.F. Holding's January Revenue Rises 8%
data.description:
S.F. Holding's revenue increased 7.9% to 26.3 billion yuan in January from 24.4 billion yuan in the year-ago period, according to a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

Revenue from its express logistics business rose 6.5% to 20.8 billion yuan from 19.5 billion yuan.

On a per-parcel basis, revenue slipped 8.2% to 15.61 yuan from 17 yuan a year earlier. Parcel volume jumped 16% to 1.33 billion from 1.15 billion a year earlier.

Revenue from its supply chain and international business operations jumped 14% to 5.51 billion yuan from 4.86 billion yuan.

Shares in S.F. Holding rose 2% in Shenzhen and 1% in Hong Kong during Thursday's afternoon trading.

data.title: Cambi Books Order for Thermal Hydrolysis Process System in Spain
data.description:
Cambi said Wednesday it received an order for a thermal hydrolysis process system from a consortium comprising Espina, Obrascon, and Aquambiente.

The system will be installed between two digestion stages at the new Silvouta wastewater treatment plant in Santiago de Compostela, Spain.

The Norwegian waste treatment technology company expects to deliver the system in 2026.
